description: Gaussian product fidelity kernel is exactly RBF k=exp(-gamma||x-x'||^2); exact Var=(1+8 g s^2)^{-M/2}-(1+4 g s^2)^{-M}; per-mode rate c=0.5 ln(1+8 g s^2); exponential fits R2 0.98-1.00
effective bandwidth gamma=eta disp^2/v_x with dgamma/deta=disp^2/v_x^2>0; per-mode concentration rate decreases with loss (e.g. r=0.7: eta=1.0 c=1.755, eta=0.1 c=0.312); increases with squeezing
loss-induced bandwidth loss exactly compensated by displacement rescaling; disp_needed 1.00 (eta1.0) to 6.12 (eta0.1); residual variance mismatch <=1e-21
GBS photon-number feature kernel (cosine), M<=3, thewalrus; concentrates more slowly in M than the Gaussian kernel at the scales studied; loss reduces GBS variance

squeezed-single-photon kernel: Wigner negativity N>0 for eta>~0.5 (threshold located between 0.50 and 0.52; N grid-converged to <0.001) and N~0 below; per-mode concentration rate smooth and positive across all eta incl. simulable regime; boundaries diverge; robust r in {0.3,0.5,1.0}
non-Gaussian product kernel exact Var vs M to M=50 via moments^M; exponential concentration persists in the classically simulable regime (eta=0.4)

validation: Gaussian kernel vs qutip (incl beamsplitter loss) 1.5e-7; exact moments vs quadrature 1.4e-15; thewalrus photon-number vs qutip 1.2e-14; non-Gaussian displacement shortcut vs direct fidelity 4.1e-9; 40/40 tests pass
